businessModel: Planet Labs PBC (NYSE: PL) operates the world's largest fleet of Earth-observation satellites and monetizes the imagery and analytics primarily through recurring data subscriptions plus a fast-growing 'Satellite Services' model in which sovereign governments fund dedicated/owned satellite capacity on Planet's next-gen Pelican fleet. ~98% of end-of-FY2026 annual contract value (ACV) is recurring. Revenue mix is increasingly weighted to government (defense and intelligence) customers, complemented by commercial verticals (agriculture, mapping, forestry, energy) and an emerging AI/analytics layer that turns raw imagery into analysis-ready insights. FY2026 (ended Jan 31, 2026) was Planet's first full year of non-GAAP (adjusted EBITDA) profitability.

segments.name: Core data subscriptions (PlanetScope / SkySat)
segments.detail: Recurring subscription access to PlanetScope near-daily global imagery (Dove/SuperDove fleet) and SkySat high-resolution tasking imagery. ~98% of end-of-FY2026 ACV is recurring; total backlog reached ~$900M (+79% YoY).

segments.name: Satellite Services (sovereign / dedicated capacity)
segments.detail: Multi-year deals where governments fund dedicated capacity and direct-downlink on Planet's next-gen Pelican fleet plus access to PlanetScope/SkySat data and AI. Three sovereign deals signed within 12 months: Germany (~EUR 240M), Japan/SKY Perfect JSAT ($230M, 10 Pelican satellites), and Sweden (low nine-figure). German revenue recognition expected to begin January 2026.

segments.name: AI / analytics ('planetary intelligence')
segments.detail: Analytics layer converting imagery into insights (object detection, anomaly/pattern recognition, maritime domain awareness). Building a 'GPU-native AI engine' with NVIDIA (Blackwell, IGX Thor) and an LLM partnership with Anthropic; selected as a prime contractor for the U.S. Missile Defense Agency SHIELD program for AI-based detection.

segments.name: Hyperspectral (Tanager / Carbon Mapper)
segments.detail: Tanager hyperspectral satellites (VSWIR imaging spectrometer designed by NASA JPL) built with the Carbon Mapper coalition; Tanager-1 launched Aug 2024 and detects methane point sources below 100 kg/hr. New Tanager spacecraft agreement signed Apr 2026.
segments.source: Business Wire (Planet and Carbon Mapper new Tanager agreement)

products: PlanetScope - near-daily global medium-resolution imagery from the Dove/SuperDove fleet (~3m)
SkySat - high-resolution (~sub-meter) tasking imagery and video

Pelican - next-generation high-resolution satellites carrying an onboard NVIDIA GPU (Jetson Orin) for edge AI and satellite-to-satellite comms; successor to SkySat (Pelican-2 launched Jan 2025; additional Pelicans launched through 2025)
Tanager - hyperspectral satellites (with Carbon Mapper) for methane/CO2 and materials detection
Planetary Variables / analytics feeds and AI insight products
Sovereign 'Satellite Services' - dedicated/owned capacity and direct downlink for governments
marketPositioning: Positioned as the leading commercial daily-imaging Earth-observation provider and an emerging 'planetary intelligence' / AI-analytics company. Increasingly a key dual-use partner for NATO and allied nations seeking sovereign space capability without building constellations from scratch, differentiated by speed (first satellites within months of contract signing vs. traditional aerospace timelines), an existing operational fleet, a deep daily global archive, vertical integration, and onboard/edge AI.
competitors: ICEYE (SAR; co-awarded alongside Planet in Sweden)
endMarkets: Defense and intelligence / national security (largest growth driver)
Commercial enterprise analytics and AI training data
growthDrivers: Sovereign 'Satellite Services' deals with allied governments amid elevated global defense spending (Germany ~EUR 240M, Japan/JSAT $230M, Sweden low nine-figure; >$500M combined)
AI/analytics monetization via the NVIDIA GPU-native engine and the Anthropic LLM partnership
Pelican high-resolution fleet ramp expanding beyond SkySat
Tanager hyperspectral expansion with Carbon Mapper
Project Suncatcher with Google - launching Planet satellites carrying Google TPUs to demo compute in space (pilot targeted ~early 2027)
Backlog conversion (~$900M, +79% YoY) into recurring revenue
customers: Swedish Armed Forces / FMV (low nine-figure deal, Jan 2026)
SKY Perfect JSAT - Japan ($230M, 10 Pelican satellites)
U.S. Missile Defense Agency (SHIELD prime contractor)
U.S. and allied defense & intelligence agencies
Agriculture, forestry and commercial analytics customers

leadership.name: Will Marshall
leadership.title: Co-Founder, Chief Executive Officer & Chairperson
leadership.background: Co-founded Planet; former NASA scientist. Leads the company's strategy around daily global imaging and the pivot to AI-driven 'planetary intelligence' and sovereign satellite services.
leadership.source: Business Wire (Planet FY2026 results, CEO quote)

leadership.name: Ashley Fieglein Johnson
leadership.title: President & Chief Financial Officer
leadership.background: Joined Planet as CFO in Feb 2020, added COO in Mar 2021, and assumed the President title in Mar 2024. Previously CFO/COO at Wealthfront and held CFO / Chief Customer Officer / Interim CEO roles at ServiceSource.
